news.headline: Alperen Sengun sorry for sexist remark at female official
news.description: Rockets center Alperen Sengun expressed remorse for calling a female referee a 'b----' earlier this week and said he apologized to her after the game.

news.headline: Hawks' new forward Jonathan Kuminga out through All-Star break
news.description: The Atlanta Hawks' newly acquired forward Jonathan Kuminga will miss at least one week while recovering from a left knee bone bruise.

news.headline: Nuggets' Cameron Johnson returns from six-week absence
news.description: Cameron Johnson is returning from a six-week absence right in time to replace Peyton Watson, who is now sidelined because of a right hamstring strain.

news.headline: Eason scores 26, Sengun gets triple-double as Rockets beat short-handed Thunder 112-106
news.description: — Tari Eason scored a season-high 26 points, and the Houston Rockets beat the short-handed Oklahoma City Thunder 112-106 on Saturday.

news.headline: Rockets pick up road win over the Thunder
news.description: The Rockets get a big-time road win over the Thunder as they snap a two-game skid

news.headline: Nets score 80 points in the first half and beat the short-handed Wizards 127-113
news.description: — Michael Porter Jr. scored 23 points and the Brooklyn Nets used an 80-point first half to beat the short-handed Washington Wizards 127-113 on Saturday.
